svg.text: Multiplexed TCP/UDP relay over interactive SSH shell sessions
Single command — bash parses in cooked mode:
FrameReader discards: command echo, python startup, ANSI
Feed raw text → extract framed messages

g.text: Terminal as transport — The PTY channel carries structured messages, not interactive commands.
Text survives everything — Base64 payloads pass through any encoding, line discipline, or character set.
Noise is invisible — Sentinels make framing unambiguous. The FrameReader ignores everything else.
Payload agnostic — The protocol carries bytes. SNMP, SSH, HTTP, SQL — all the same.
Zero-install remote — Two Python files, stdlib only, Python 3.7+. scp and run.
Serialized writes — ChannelWriter prevents frame interleaving from concurrent streams.
